occupation: Full Stack Java Developer at Acentrik
headline: Software Developer | C#, Java, Python, .NET Core | Cloud (AWS, Azure) & DevOps (Docker, Kubernetes) | React, PostgreSQL, Streamlit
summary:
As a passionate and dedicated .NET Developer with over four years of professional experience, I bring a unique blend of technical expertise and innovative problem-solving across software development, machine learning, and digital technologies. My Master's in Computer Science from Wright State University underpins my comprehensive skill set, spanning C# programming, web development, and cutting-edge technologies like cloud computing and machine learning. 

I specialize in transforming complex business requirements into robust technological solutions, leveraging my proficiency in .NET Core, ASP.NET, Entity Framework, LINQ, React, and TypeScript to design scalable enterprise applications that drive operational efficiency. Through pivotal projects like my Inventory Management System and Employee Management System, I've consistently delivered impactful solutions, reducing API response times by 30%, improving database query performance by 20%, and decreasing data inconsistencies by 15%. 

My technical versatility extends beyond coding to include cloud platforms, DevOps practices, and microservices architecture, with proven success in implementing role-based access control systems and optimizing digital campaigns. Complementing my technical skills, I hold certifications in Google Ads and Machine Learning, demonstrating my ability to extract meaningful insights and create targeted solutions. 

I excel in collaborative environments, continuously learn emerging technologies, and am committed to driving innovation through strategic technological approaches. Whether it's developing complex software systems, analyzing data, or creating engaging digital campaigns, I bring a holistic and innovative approach to solving technological challenges.

experiences.title: Full Stack Java Developer
experiences.description: Assist in developing and deploying web applications using Java and related technologies. Create responsive front-end interfaces and integrate with back-end systems. Collaborate with cross-functional teams in an agile environment. Document technical processes, troubleshoot and provide support for deployed applications.

education.description: Skills: Communication · Programming · Coding Standards · Amazon Web Services (AWS) · Data Structures · Deep Learning · Agile Application Development · Python (Programming Language) · Machine Learning · Computer Science · Problem Solving · Data Analysis · Exploratory Data Analysis · Java · Agile Environment · Engineering
Skills: Deep Learning · Exploratory Data Analysis · Engineering

accomplishment_projects.title: Inventory Management System
accomplishment_projects.description:
The 𝗜𝗻𝘃𝗲𝗻𝘁𝗼𝗿𝘆 𝗠𝗮𝗻𝗮𝗴𝗲𝗺𝗲𝗻𝘁 𝗦𝘆𝘀𝘁𝗲𝗺 (𝗜𝗠𝗦) is a web application designed to streamline the management of products, suppliers, and stock levels. Built with .𝗡𝗘𝗧 𝗖𝗼𝗿𝗲 for the backend and 𝗥𝗲𝗮𝗰𝘁𝗝𝗦 for the frontend, it provides a scalable and user-friendly platform for modern businesses. The backend uses 𝗔𝗦𝗣.𝗡𝗘𝗧 𝗖𝗼𝗿𝗲 for 𝗥𝗘𝗦𝗧𝗳𝘂𝗹 𝗔𝗣𝗜𝘀, 𝗘𝗻𝘁𝗶𝘁𝘆 𝗙𝗿𝗮𝗺𝗲𝘄𝗼𝗿𝗸 𝗖𝗼𝗿𝗲 for database interactions, and 𝗔𝘇𝘂𝗿𝗲 𝗦𝗤𝗟 Server for secure data storage. The frontend leverages ReactJS to create a responsive and interactive user interface, complemented by CSS and styled components for a clean design. Together, the technologies enable seamless integration between the frontend and backend, offering features such as product and supplier management, stock tracking, and efficient data handling.  

The Inventory Management System (IMS) boasts a modular architecture and 𝗵𝗶𝗴𝗵 𝗽𝗲𝗿𝗳𝗼𝗿𝗺𝗮𝗻𝗰𝗲, with under 𝟮𝟬𝟬𝗺𝘀 response times for critical APIs. 𝗖𝗼𝗱𝗲 𝗰𝗼𝘃𝗲𝗿𝗮𝗴𝗲 exceeds 𝟴𝟱%, ensuring high code quality. It streamlines inventory workflows, reduces costs, and improves order fulfillment. The IMS is a robust and efficient solution for businesses seeking to optimize their inventory management processes."

accomplishment_projects.title: Weather Image Classification
accomplishment_projects.description:
🌦️ Weather Image Classification Project 🌈  

This project focuses on classifying weather phenomena using a dataset containing over 6,800 labeled images across 11 categories, including hail, rainbow, frost, snow, and more. Through exploratory data analysis and data visualization, I uncovered insights into the dataset's structure, class distribution, and image characteristics.  

Key highlights of the project:  
- Conducted exploratory data analysis (EDA) to identify class imbalances and visualize image distributions.  
- Designed intuitive visualizations using Matplotlib, Seaborn, and Plotly Express for effective communication of findings.  
- Processed images with Pillow (PIL) and utilized Pandas for data management and preparation.  
- Gained hands-on experience working with a real-world image dataset and preparing it for machine learning applications.

accomplishment_projects.title: Financial Intelligence Platform
accomplishment_projects.description:
The 𝗙𝗶𝗻𝗮𝗻𝗰𝗶𝗮𝗹 𝗜𝗻𝘁𝗲𝗹𝗹𝗶𝗴𝗲𝗻𝗰𝗲 𝗣𝗹𝗮𝘁𝗳𝗼𝗿𝗺 is a cutting-edge tool designed to provide actionable insights by transforming complex market data. It features 𝗿𝗲𝗮𝗹-𝘁𝗶𝗺𝗲 𝘀𝗲𝗻𝘁𝗶𝗺𝗲𝗻𝘁 𝗮𝗻𝗮𝗹𝘆𝘀𝗶𝘀 from multiple sources, including news aggregation and social media trends, alongside 𝗺𝗮𝗰𝗵𝗶𝗻𝗲 𝗹𝗲𝗮𝗿𝗻𝗶𝗻𝗴 𝗽𝗿𝗲𝗱𝗶𝗰𝘁𝗶𝗼𝗻𝘀 𝗳𝗼𝗿 𝘀𝘁𝗼𝗰𝗸 𝗽𝗲𝗿𝗳𝗼𝗿𝗺𝗮𝗻𝗰𝗲, market trend forecasting, and risk assessments. Interactive visualizations, such as 𝗿𝗲𝗮𝗹-𝘁𝗶𝗺𝗲 𝗱𝗮𝘀𝗵𝗯𝗼𝗮𝗿𝗱𝘀, custom technical indicators, and sentiment gauge charts, help users understand market dynamics. The platform also employs 𝗻𝗮𝘁𝘂𝗿𝗮𝗹 𝗹𝗮𝗻𝗴𝘂𝗮𝗴𝗲 𝗽𝗿𝗼𝗰𝗲𝘀𝘀𝗶𝗻𝗴 for trend analysis and 𝘀𝗲𝗻𝘁𝗶𝗺𝗲𝗻𝘁 𝗰𝗹𝗮𝘀𝘀𝗶𝗳𝗶𝗰𝗮𝘁𝗶𝗼𝗻 from news articles. Built using 𝗣𝘆𝘁𝗵𝗼𝗻 𝟯.𝟭𝟭, 𝗙𝗹𝗮𝘀𝗸, 𝗦𝘁𝗿𝗲𝗮𝗺𝗹𝗶𝘁, and 𝗣𝗼𝘀𝘁𝗴𝗿𝗲𝗦𝗤𝗟, it integrates market data from 𝘆𝗳𝗶𝗻𝗮𝗻𝗰𝗲, 𝗦𝗘𝗖 𝗔𝗣𝗜, and 𝗙𝗶𝗻𝗻𝗵𝘂𝗯.

This platform enhances financial decision-making by offering a sentiment analysis dashboard, portfolio management tools for performance tracking, and ML-based market predictions. The tool has demonstrated a 𝟯𝟬% 𝗶𝗺𝗽𝗿𝗼𝘃𝗲𝗺𝗲𝗻𝘁 𝗶𝗻 𝗽𝗿𝗲𝗱𝗶𝗰𝘁𝗶𝗼𝗻 𝗮𝗰𝗰𝘂𝗿𝗮𝗰𝘆 and a 𝟮𝟬% 𝗳𝗮𝘀𝘁𝗲𝗿 𝗽𝗿𝗼𝗰𝗲𝘀𝘀𝗶𝗻𝗴 𝘁𝗶𝗺𝗲 for market data. Users can access the platform locally by installing dependencies, setting up API keys, and running the 𝗦𝘁𝗿𝗲𝗮𝗺𝗹𝗶𝘁 app at 𝗹𝗼𝗰𝗮𝗹𝗵𝗼𝘀𝘁:𝟱𝟬𝟬𝟬. Designed for financial professionals, the platform delivers real-time insights, predictive analytics, and risk management solutions to stay ahead in a fast-moving market.
